qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: JWT signature verification bypass via none algorithm
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Identical vulnerability: same library (jose-swift), same vulnerable code (JWS+Verify.swift:35 guard statement returning true for alg:none), same root cause (accepting 'none' algorithm without signature verification), same impact (authentication bypass via forged JWTs). The finding explicitly references GHSA-88q6-jcjg-hvmw and the vulnerable commit.

selected_finding.title: JWT signature verification bypass via none algorithm

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The jws-none-algorithm-bypass finding perfectly matches the advisory-described vulnerability: same library (jose-swift), same vulnerable code location (Sources/JSONWebSignature/JWS+Verify.swift:35), same root cause (guard statement returning true for SigningAlgorithm.none without verification), same impact (authentication bypass via forged JWTs). The finding explicitly references GHSA-88q6-jcjg-hvmw and provides strong static evidence including the vulnerable guard statement, the none algorithm enum case, and test code demonstrating the bypass. PoC execution failed due to Docker network timeout when pulling swift:5.9-jammy base image, preventing runtime verification.
evidence: Finding identifies the exact vulnerable code location: Sources/JSONWebSignature/JWS+Verify.swift:35-37
Finding describes the same root cause: guard SigningAlgorithm.none != protectedHeader.algorithm else { return true }
Finding references the same advisory: GHSA-88q6-jcjg-hvmw
Finding references the same vulnerable commit: f77adcffb85fc9dc4f3aa5059e4777f5a7bf35d6
Finding describes the same attack vector: forged JWT with alg:none and empty signature
Finding describes the same impact: authentication bypass, arbitrary user impersonation, privilege escalation
poc_report.json confirms the vulnerability was previously reproduced with the proof string EVOHUNT_NONE_BYPASS_CONFIRMED
blockers_to_higher_tier: PoC execution failed due to Docker network timeout when pulling swift:5.9-jammy base image
Runtime verification could not be completed to satisfy exploit_success_predicate or bug_activation_predicate
Environment setup failure (network connectivity) prevented reaching the vulnerable code path

poc_executions.observed_effect: Docker build failed while attempting to pull swift:5.9-jammy base image from docker.io. Network timeout error: 'dial tcp 174.36.228.136:443: i/o timeout'. The vulnerable code path was never reached due to this environment setup failure.
